  "title": "Facility Manager",
  "description": "Welcome to Studyhabit School, Lagos. Top ranking college, Primary and Nursery School in Nigeria. We are a government approved academic institution operating across multiple campuses in the Ago-Okota/Isolo area. Offering classes from Playgroup through Year 12, it blends Nigerian and British (Cambridge) curricula with Montessori foundations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: &nbsp;Facility Manager

Job Location: Ago Place Way, Okota - Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Qualifications


	Interested candidate(s) should possess relevant qualifications and experience.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: hr@studyhabitschool.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
Or&nbsp;
Send hard copies of their CV &amp; handwritten Cover Letter to
119-129, Alhaji Lateef Aregbe Street,
Ago-Okota,
Lagos State.
OR
16-20, Owolabi Street,
Ago Palace,
Ago-Okota,
Lagos State.

Note: Shortlisted applicants will be taken through our recruitment process which will commence first week of July. Successful candidates will be expected to resume with us on the 1st of September 2026.",

Facility Manager Job at Studyhabit School




        
    

        
        Welcome to Studyhabit School, Lagos. Top ranking college, Primary and Nursery School in Nigeria. We are a government approved academic institution operating across multiple campuses in the Ago-Okota/Isolo area. Offering classes from Playgroup through Year 12, it blends Nigerian and British (Cambridge) curricula with Montessori foundations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:  Facility Manager

Job Location: Ago Place Way, Okota - Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Qualifications
     

            
    




	Interested candidate(s) should possess relevant qualifications and experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
Or 
Send hard copies of their CV & handwritten Cover Letter to
119-129, Alhaji Lateef Aregbe Street,
Ago-Okota,
Lagos State.
OR
16-20, Owolabi Street,
Ago Palace,
Ago-Okota,
Lagos State.

Note: Shortlisted applicants will be taken through our recruitment process which will commence first week of July. Successful candidates will be expected to resume with us on the 1st of September 2026.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Safety Manager",
  "description": "Linkup Human Capital Limited is an indigenous, leading management consulting firm in Nigeria, a workforce solutions provider that renders professional services and solutions in areas that span multiple industries and has been immensely successful in creating a global network of highly adept intelligent workforce that can help a company achieve its mission/critical projects and goals. We focus on delivering value to organizations, which in turn increases the bottom line of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Safety Manager

Job Location: Epe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and monitor HSE policies, procedures, and safety management systems.
	Ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, and environmental regulations and company standards.
	Conduct routine workplace inspections, risk assessments, and safety audits to identify and mitigate hazards.
	Lead incident investigations, identify root causes, and implement corrective and preventive actions.
	Organize safety induction, training, and awareness programmes for employees and contractors.
	Monitor the proper use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and ensure adherence to safe work practices.
	Prepare and submit HSE reports, safety statistics, and regulatory documentation.
	Coordinate emergency preparedness activities, including drills and response plans.
	Work closely with production and maintenance teams to promote a safe working environment and continuous improvement.
	Foster a positive safety culture through employee engagement and regular safety campaigns.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Engineering, Environmental Science, Occupational Health &amp; Safety, or a related discipline.
	5&ndash;7years of proven HSE experience, preferably in a manufacturing environment; experience in furniture or wood-processing industries is an added advantage.
	Professional HSE certification (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, OSHA, or equivalent) is highly desirable.
	Strong knowledge of occupational health, safety, and environmental regulations.
	Experience conducting risk assessments, incident investigations, and safety audits.
	Excellent leadership, communication, problem-solving, and report-writing skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure and influence a strong safety culture across all levels of the organization.


Salary
Attractive and Negotiable.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: careers@linkuphcl.com using &quot;Safety Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Safety Manager Job at Linkup Human Capital Limited




        
    

        
        Linkup Human Capital Limited is an indigenous, leading management consulting firm in Nigeria, a workforce solutions provider that renders professional services and solutions in areas that span multiple industries and has been immensely successful in creating a global network of highly adept intelligent workforce that can help a company achieve its mission/critical projects and goals. We focus on delivering value to organizations, which in turn increases the bottom line of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Safety Manager

Job Location: Epe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Develop, implement, and monitor HSE policies, procedures, and safety management systems.
	Ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, and environmental regulations and company standards.
	Conduct routine workplace inspections, risk assessments, and safety audits to identify and mitigate hazards.
	Lead incident investigations, identify root causes, and implement corrective and preventive actions.
	Organize safety induction, training, and awareness programmes for employees and contractors.
	Monitor the proper use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and ensure adherence to safe work practices.
	Prepare and submit HSE reports, safety statistics, and regulatory documentation.
	Coordinate emergency preparedness activities, including drills and response plans.
	Work closely with production and maintenance teams to promote a safe working environment and continuous improvement.
	Foster a positive safety culture through employee engagement and regular safety campaigns.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Engineering, Environmental Science, Occupational Health & Safety, or a related discipline.
	5–7years of proven HSE experience, preferably in a manufacturing environment; experience in furniture or wood-processing industries is an added advantage.
	Professional HSE certification (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, OSHA, or equivalent) is highly desirable.
	Strong knowledge of occupational health, safety, and environmental regulations.
	Experience conducting risk assessments, incident investigations, and safety audits.
	Excellent leadership, communication, problem-solving, and report-writing skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to work effectively under pressure and influence a strong safety culture across all levels of the organization.


Salary
Attractive and Negotiable.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using "Safety Manager" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Treasury Manager",
  "description": "ZitraPay is a B2B cross-border payments company built for businesses that move money internationally. Operating at the intersection of compliance, technology, and financial infrastructure, ZitraPay specialises in international invoice settlements and Payment on Behalf of (PoBo) services; enabling companies to pay foreign vendors, suppliers, and partners efficiently across multiple corridors.

ZitraPay is a B2B cross-border payments company helping businesses settle international payments with speed, precision, and competitive rates. We serve importers, distributors, and corporates making payments to vendors and suppliers globally; with a primary focus on the USD/NGN corridor and growing corridors across Africa, Asia, and the Americas.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Treasury Manager

Job Location: Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are looking for a Treasury Manager who understands that treasury is a revenue function &amp; not just a cost or rate-matching centre. This person knows how to source and manage liquidity, reads FX markets, matches rates intelligently, and builds the partnerships that make our pricing sharper and our rails faster.
	You will own ZitraPay&#39;s treasury operations end-to-end: liquidity, banking relationships, payment rails, and commercial fintech partnerships. This is a high-ownership role reporting directly to the founder, and the right candidate will have visible impact from day one.


Key Responsibilities
Liquidity &amp; FX Management:


	Source, evaluate, and onboard competitive fiat and stablecoin liquidity providers and OTC desks.
	Manage rate-matching strategy to ensure ZitraPay&#39;s pricing remains competitive without eroding margin.


Banking &amp; Payment Infrastructure:


	Establish and deepen cross-border banking relationships with a focus on lower fees, faster settlement, and greater flexibility.
	Identify and onboard new payment rails across priority African markets including Ghana, Senegal, Kenya, The Gambia, and Cameroon.


Commercial &amp; Fintech Partnerships:


	Identify and structure cross-trade partnerships with other fintechs; positioning ZitraPay as a liquidity provider where our pricing and infrastructure creates mutual advantage.
	Hunt new commercial partners systematically; understand their pain points and map ZitraPay&#39;s strengths to their needs (e.g, Oil &amp; gas, Multinations, major Pharmaceuticals and some major Stablecoin buyers).
	Maintain a live pipeline of active and prospective LP and commercial partner relationships.
	Support treasury management services for multinational clients seeking FX solutions and liquidity support.


Operations &amp; Reporting:


	Maintain accurate records of all treasury positions, trades, and partner arrangements (i.e trade trackers &amp; payment blotter).
	Produce regular reporting on FX performance, ratios, spread analysis, and Profi &amp; Loss.


Expectations &amp; Requirements


	2&ndash;5 years of experience in FX trading, treasury operations, or a liquidity-focused role within a fintech, OTC desk, or financial institution.
	Demonstrated ability to source and negotiate with liquidity providers &mdash; you have existing relationships or know how to build them quickly.
	Strong FX market knowledge with a working understanding of NGN, USD, and at least one other African currency corridor.
	Commercial instinct: you understand the difference between generating revenue through spreads and simply marking up rates.
	Analytically sharp with a hands-on approach to data; comfortable building and maintaining Excel dashboards, rate calculators, and spread/volume trackers from scratch. You don&#39;t wait for a system to tell you the numbers; you build the tools that surface them.
	Experience building or managing cross-border banking and payment relationships.
	Comfortable operating in a lean, fast-moving environment where you own the outcome.
	Existing network within the African or diaspora fintech/OTC space is a strong advantage.
	Familiarity with stablecoin liquidity and digital asset rails is a compulsory requirement.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,500,000 / Month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a a concise cover-letter telling us why this role fits where you are in your career, and how you can contribute to us significantly to: business@zitrapay.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	We don&#39;t need a cover letter that re-summarises your resume.
	You can add a liquidity deal or partnership you built &mdash; what you did, what you negotiated, and what it delivered.

Treasury Manager Job at ZitraPay




        
    

        
        ZitraPay is a B2B cross-border payments company built for businesses that move money internationally. Operating at the intersection of compliance, technology, and financial infrastructure, ZitraPay specialises in international invoice settlements and Payment on Behalf of (PoBo) services; enabling companies to pay foreign vendors, suppliers, and partners efficiently across multiple corridors.

ZitraPay is a B2B cross-border payments company helping businesses settle international payments with speed, precision, and competitive rates. We serve importers, distributors, and corporates making payments to vendors and suppliers globally; with a primary focus on the USD/NGN corridor and growing corridors across Africa, Asia, and the Americas.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Treasury Manager

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are looking for a Treasury Manager who understands that treasury is a revenue function & not just a cost or rate-matching centre. This person knows how to source and manage liquidity, reads FX markets, matches rates intelligently, and builds the partnerships that make our pricing sharper and our rails faster.
	You will own ZitraPay's treasury operations end-to-end: liquidity, banking relationships, payment rails, and commercial fintech partnerships. This is a high-ownership role reporting directly to the founder, and the right candidate will have visible impact from day one.


Key Responsibilities
Liquidity & FX Management:


	Source, evaluate, and onboard competitive fiat and stablecoin liquidity providers and OTC desks.
	Manage rate-matching strategy to ensure ZitraPay's pricing remains competitive without eroding margin.


Banking & Payment Infrastructure:


	Establish and deepen cross-border banking relationships with a focus on lower fees, faster settlement, and greater flexibility.
	Identify and onboard new payment rails across priority African markets including Ghana, Senegal, Kenya, The Gambia, and Cameroon.


Commercial & Fintech Partnerships:


	Identify and structure cross-trade partnerships with other fintechs; positioning ZitraPay as a liquidity provider where our pricing and infrastructure creates mutual advantage.
	Hunt new commercial partners systematically; understand their pain points and map ZitraPay's strengths to their needs (e.g, Oil & gas, Multinations, major Pharmaceuticals and some major Stablecoin buyers).
	Maintain a live pipeline of active and prospective LP and commercial partner relationships.
	Support treasury management services for multinational clients seeking FX solutions and liquidity support.


Operations & Reporting:


	Maintain accurate records of all treasury positions, trades, and partner arrangements (i.e trade trackers & payment blotter).
	Produce regular reporting on FX performance, ratios, spread analysis, and Profi & Loss.


Expectations & Requirements


	2–5 years of experience in FX trading, treasury operations, or a liquidity-focused role within a fintech, OTC desk, or financial institution.
	Demonstrated ability to source and negotiate with liquidity providers — you have existing relationships or know how to build them quickly.
	Strong FX market knowledge with a working understanding of NGN, USD, and at least one other African currency corridor.
	Commercial instinct: you understand the difference between generating revenue through spreads and simply marking up rates.
	Analytically sharp with a hands-on approach to data; comfortable building and maintaining Excel dashboards, rate calculators, and spread/volume trackers from scratch. You don't wait for a system to tell you the numbers; you build the tools that surface them.
	Experience building or managing cross-border banking and payment relationships.
	Comfortable operating in a lean, fast-moving environment where you own the outcome.
	Existing network within the African or diaspora fintech/OTC space is a strong advantage.
	Familiarity with stablecoin liquidity and digital asset rails is a compulsory requirement.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,500,000 / Month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a a concise cover-letter telling us why this role fits where you are in your career, and how you can contribute to us significantly to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	We don't need a cover letter that re-summarises your resume.
	You can add a liquidity deal or partnership you built — what you did, what you negotiated, and what it delivered.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Human Edge Dimension Consulting is a modern HR consulting firm dedicated to helping startups, small businesses, and growing enterprises build strong human capital foundations. Founded in 2022, our company was born out of a clear need: to make expert-level HR support accessible, affordable, and impactful for businesses in their early growth stages.

We bring a fresh, innovative perspective to HR strategy&mdash;combining legal compliance with people-first practices. Whether you&#39;re hiring your first employee or restructuring a growing team, we deliver flexible solutions that evolve with your business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Backend Developer

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to: CTO

Job Overview


	Backend Developer is to design, develop, and maintain scalable server-side applications.
	The ideal candidate should have strong experience in Laravel and working knowledge of Node.js for building efficient APIs and backend services.


Responsibilities


	Design, develop, test, and maintain backend applications and APIs.
	Build and manage databases, data models, and server-side architecture.
	Develop secure and scalable solutions that support business growth.
	Integrate third-party services, payment gateways, and external APIs as required.
	Optimize application performance, reliability, and responsiveness.
	Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve software defects and technical issues.
	Implement security and data protection measures in line with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Collaborate with front-end developers to ensure seamless system integration.
	Develop and maintain technical documentation for applications and systems.
	Participate in code reviews and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives.
	Monitor system performance and recommend enhancements where necessary,
	Ensure applications are properly tested before deployment to production environments.


Position Specification
Key Requirements:
Qualifications:


	A minimum of a Bachelor&#39;s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent experience


Work Experience:


	At least 3- 5 years of work experience as a backend developer.


Technical Knowledge Area:


	Strong proficiency in PHP and Laravel framework
	Experience building RESTful APIs
	Solid understanding of database design (MySQL/PostgreSQL)
	Familiarity with authentication systems (JWT, sessions)
	Experience with version control (Git)
	Working knowledge of Node.js
	Experience with frameworks such as Express.js or NestJS is an advantage
	Understanding of API integration and third-party services
	Basic knowledge of server management (Linux, Nginx)
	Familiarity with Docker is a plus


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Application to: humanedgeconsulting02@gmail.com using the Position as the subject of the mail",

Backend Developer Job at Human Edge Dimension Consulting




        
    

        
        Human Edge Dimension Consulting is a modern HR consulting firm dedicated to helping startups, small businesses, and growing enterprises build strong human capital foundations. Founded in 2022, our company was born out of a clear need: to make expert-level HR support accessible, affordable, and impactful for businesses in their early growth stages.

We bring a fresh, innovative perspective to HR strategy—combining legal compliance with people-first practices. Whether you're hiring your first employee or restructuring a growing team, we deliver flexible solutions that evolve with your business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Backend Developer
     

            
    



Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to: CTO

Job Overview


	Backend Developer is to design, develop, and maintain scalable server-side applications.
	The ideal candidate should have strong experience in Laravel and working knowledge of Node.js for building efficient APIs and backend services.


Responsibilities


	Design, develop, test, and maintain backend applications and APIs.
	Build and manage databases, data models, and server-side architecture.
	Develop secure and scalable solutions that support business growth.
	Integrate third-party services, payment gateways, and external APIs as required.
	Optimize application performance, reliability, and responsiveness.
	Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve software defects and technical issues.
	Implement security and data protection measures in line with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Collaborate with front-end developers to ensure seamless system integration.
	Develop and maintain technical documentation for applications and systems.
	Participate in code reviews and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives.
	Monitor system performance and recommend enhancements where necessary,
	Ensure applications are properly tested before deployment to production environments.


Position Specification
Key Requirements:
Qualifications:


	A minimum of a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent experience


Work Experience:


	At least 3- 5 years of work experience as a backend developer.


Technical Knowledge Area:


	Strong proficiency in PHP and Laravel framework
	Experience building RESTful APIs
	Solid understanding of database design (MySQL/PostgreSQL)
	Familiarity with authentication systems (JWT, sessions)
	Experience with version control (Git)
	Working knowledge of Node.js
	Experience with frameworks such as Express.js or NestJS is an advantage
	Understanding of API integration and third-party services
	Basic knowledge of server management (Linux, Nginx)
	Familiarity with Docker is a plus


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Application to: [email protected] using the Position as the subject of the mail    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "IT Specialist",
  "description": "Itel Energy is a renewable energy solutions company focused on providing reliable, affordable, and sustainable solar power solutions across Nigeria. As part of the itel ecosystem, Itel Energy leverages strong distribution networks and deep market reach to deliver innovative off-grid and hybrid solar solutions for homes, SMEs, and commercial customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Specialist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
Desktop and End-User Support:


	Provide daily support and maintenance for company desktops, laptops, printers, projectors, and other peripheral devices.
	Install, configure, reinstall, and troubleshoot operating systems, office software, browsers, and other commonly used applications.
	Support IT setup for employee onboarding, internal transfers, and offboarding, including workstation preparation, account setup, and IT asset collection.


Network and Infrastructure Support:


	Maintain and support the company&rsquo;s LAN, Wi-Fi, VPN, firewalls, routers, switches, and other network infrastructure.
	Troubleshoot network issues, manage IP allocation, and support office cabling and connectivity-related work.
	Coordinate the maintenance and support of low-voltage systems, including access control, CCTV, and office telephony where applicable.


Business Applications and Account Administration:


	Manage user accounts and access permissions for email, instant messaging, OA, ERP, CRM, finance systems, and other business applications.
	Provide day-to-day support for business systems and coordinate with second-line support teams or external vendors to resolve complex issues when needed.
	Support the maintenance or development of internal scripts and lightweight automation tools based on business needs, where applicable.


Information Security Support:


	Support endpoint security management, including antivirus protection, patch deployment, and control of USB devices and other peripherals.
	Assist in implementing data backup policies and conducting access control reviews and basic security checks.
	Participate in the response and handling of IT security incidents, including malware infections, ransomware, and potential data leakage issues.


IT Asset Management:


	Maintain accurate IT asset records and manage the full lifecycle of IT assets, including procurement, allocation, transfer, repair, and disposal.
	Conduct quarterly and annual IT asset inventory checks to ensure alignment between physical assets and system records.
	Support software compliance and license management.


Vendor and Procurement Support:


	Support vendor communication, quotation comparison, and procurement coordination for hardware, software, and outsourced IT services.
	Assist in tracking maintenance contracts, renewals, and service level agreement (SLA) performance.


Processes and Documentation:


	Prepare, update, and maintain IT policies, request procedures, operating guidelines, and emergency response plans.
	Build and maintain IT knowledge base materials and FAQ documentation, and provide basic IT orientation and support for new employees.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or above in Information Technology, Computer Science, Network Engineering, or a related field.
	At least 2 years of relevant experience in IT support, IT administration, system operations, or infrastructure support.
	Solid knowledge of Windows operating systems, Microsoft Office applications, common office hardware, and end-user troubleshooting.
	Familiarity with LAN, Wi-Fi, VPN, routers, switches, firewalls, and basic network administration and troubleshooting.
	Experience in user account administration and access control for enterprise systems.
	Basic understanding of endpoint security, patch management, data backup, and IT asset management.
	Good communication and coordination skills, strong service awareness, and solid problem-solving ability.
	Relevant IT certifications will be an advantage.


Method of Applictaion&nbsp;

IT Specialist Job at Itel Energy




        
    

        
        Itel Energy is a renewable energy solutions company focused on providing reliable, affordable, and sustainable solar power solutions across Nigeria. As part of the itel ecosystem, Itel Energy leverages strong distribution networks and deep market reach to deliver innovative off-grid and hybrid solar solutions for homes, SMEs, and commercial customers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Specialist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
Desktop and End-User Support:
     

            
    




	Provide daily support and maintenance for company desktops, laptops, printers, projectors, and other peripheral devices.
	Install, configure, reinstall, and troubleshoot operating systems, office software, browsers, and other commonly used applications.
	Support IT setup for employee onboarding, internal transfers, and offboarding, including workstation preparation, account setup, and IT asset collection.


Network and Infrastructure Support:


	Maintain and support the company’s LAN, Wi-Fi, VPN, firewalls, routers, switches, and other network infrastructure.
	Troubleshoot network issues, manage IP allocation, and support office cabling and connectivity-related work.
	Coordinate the maintenance and support of low-voltage systems, including access control, CCTV, and office telephony where applicable.


Business Applications and Account Administration:


	Manage user accounts and access permissions for email, instant messaging, OA, ERP, CRM, finance systems, and other business applications.
	Provide day-to-day support for business systems and coordinate with second-line support teams or external vendors to resolve complex issues when needed.
	Support the maintenance or development of internal scripts and lightweight automation tools based on business needs, where applicable.


Information Security Support:


	Support endpoint security management, including antivirus protection, patch deployment, and control of USB devices and other peripherals.
	Assist in implementing data backup policies and conducting access control reviews and basic security checks.
	Participate in the response and handling of IT security incidents, including malware infections, ransomware, and potential data leakage issues.


IT Asset Management:


	Maintain accurate IT asset records and manage the full lifecycle of IT assets, including procurement, allocation, transfer, repair, and disposal.
	Conduct quarterly and annual IT asset inventory checks to ensure alignment between physical assets and system records.
	Support software compliance and license management.


Vendor and Procurement Support:


	Support vendor communication, quotation comparison, and procurement coordination for hardware, software, and outsourced IT services.
	Assist in tracking maintenance contracts, renewals, and service level agreement (SLA) performance.


Processes and Documentation:


	Prepare, update, and maintain IT policies, request procedures, operating guidelines, and emergency response plans.
	Build and maintain IT knowledge base materials and FAQ documentation, and provide basic IT orientation and support for new employees.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree or above in Information Technology, Computer Science, Network Engineering, or a related field.
	At least 2 years of relevant experience in IT support, IT administration, system operations, or infrastructure support.
	Solid knowledge of Windows operating systems, Microsoft Office applications, common office hardware, and end-user troubleshooting.
	Familiarity with LAN, Wi-Fi, VPN, routers, switches, firewalls, and basic network administration and troubleshooting.
	Experience in user account administration and access control for enterprise systems.
	Basic understanding of endpoint security, patch management, data backup, and IT asset management.
	Good communication and coordination skills, strong service awareness, and solid problem-solving ability.
	Relevant IT certifications will be an advantage.


Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified applicants should send their CVs to [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
